articles.title: Employment Situation Summary — May 2026 (BLS)
articles.summary: Primary source. Nonfarm payrolls +172K vs ~85K consensus. Unemployment unchanged at 4.3%. March revised +29K to +214K; April revised +64K to +179K (+93K combined). Leisure/hospitality +70K (5x 12-mo avg), local gov +55K, healthcare +35K. AHE +0.3% MoM / +3.4% YoY. Long-term unemployed 2.0M (+524K YoY). Financial activities -22K (down 107K from May 2025 peak). Labor force participation 61.8%.

articles.title: Jobs Report May 2026 — CNBC
articles.summary: Payrolls crushed expectations. Market futures negative, Treasury yields surged. 'Hiring recession is over' per Navy Federal CU. U-6 rate edged lower to 8.1%. World Cup hiring likely boosted leisure sector. Fed rate cuts not on near-term horizon. GDP tracking +3% in Q2 (Atlanta Fed). Expert consensus: labor market is 'pretty darn solid' and doesn't need Fed support.

articles.title: Chip Slump Erases $1.3 Trillion in Stock Market Value — Reuters
articles.summary: SOX index -10.3%, worst single-day drop since March 2020 COVID crash. Two-day decline: -12%. NVDA -6.2% (>$300B market cap loss), MU -13%, MRVL -17%, AMD -11%, AVGO -7.9% (two-day -20%). Triggered by AVGO's AI networking revenue miss ($4.1B vs $4.8B expected). 'Blindly buying the dip had been winning you money, but that ended today' — Dennis Dick. Wells Fargo strategist: 'I don't think it's the end of the semi bull market.' SOX still +73% YTD.

articles.title: Wall Street Ends Sharply Lower as Chips Slide, Jobs Data Fuels Rate Hike Fears — Reuters
articles.summary: Dow -695 pts (-1.35%) to 50,867. S&P 500 -2.64% to 7,384 — snapped 9-week winning streak. Nasdaq -4.18% to 25,709 — worst day since April 2025. VIX surged 34% above 20. Rate hike probability for December at 42.7% (CME FedWatch). Iran war overhang. LULU -8.6% (cut guidance). META -7% (exploring secondary offering). S&P won't change eligibility rules — no quick S&P 500 entry for SpaceX. 22.89B shares traded vs. 20.29B average.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Falls 4% as Semiconductor Slide Wipes $1T — TheStreet
articles.summary: Over $1T in market value erased. Russell 2000 -3.47%. 20Y/30Y yields above 5%. Meta exploring billions in new share issuance days after Alphabet's $80B raise. Amazon unveils next-gen Proteus warehouse robot with conversational AI at London event. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang in South Korea promoting robotics/physical AI. LULU -13% premarket on guidance cut. COO +5.1% on earnings beat. Gold -0.35% at $4,489. WTI -0.44% at $92.63. BTC -3.52% at $61,915.

articles.title: Nasdaq Suffers Worst Week in More Than a Year — WSJ
articles.summary: Nasdaq -4.2% Friday, -4.7% for the week — sharpest weekly decline in >1 year. 10Y yield jumped past 4.5%. 2Y at 4.16% — highest in a year. 70% probability of rate hike by year-end (up from <50% pre-report). Trump posted: 'With a great Jobs Report, stocks should go up, not down. Growth does not mean inflation!' Dow -0.3% for the week. S&P snapped 9-week win streak. Micron, Marvell, Intel, AMD, Qualcomm, ARM all down 10%+.

articles.title: Bitcoin Plunges Toward $60,000 — Strategy's First Sale Triggers Panic, Record ETF Outflows
articles.summary: BTC -5.24% to $60,463 — lowest since Feb 6. 12 consecutive days of spot ETF net outflows totaling $4B — worst streak since inception (Bitwise CIO). Capital rotating from crypto to AI infrastructure and SpaceX IPO anticipation. MSTR unrealized loss ~$10.8B. 'The symbolic significance of this sale far outweighs its actual impact.' Risk of negative feedback loop: falling stock → leveraged product pressure → forced BTC selling → lower prices.

articles.title: Strategy Sold 32 BTC for $2.5 Million in Late May — CoinDesk
articles.summary: Strategy sold 32 BTC at avg $77,135/coin between May 26-31. Proceeds fund STRC preferred dividends. First disclosed net BTC disposal. Holdings: 843,706 BTC at $75,699 avg cost. Sale represented 0.0038% of holdings. Saylor on Q1 call: 'We will probably sell some bitcoin to pay a dividend just to inoculate the market.' MSTR fell ~6% on announcement. BTC dropped below $71,500. $90M in BTC futures liquidated afterward.

articles.title: ICE BofA US Corporate Index OAS — FRED (St. Louis Fed)
articles.source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED)
articles.summary: Primary source. IG OAS at 0.74% (74 bps) as of June 4 — unchanged all week (73-74 bps). Near historic tights. Credit spreads not confirming equity selloff — suggests positioning unwind, not systemic event. 3-year data range: 0.7%-1.5%. Bottom decile of post-GFC readings. Next release: June 8.

articles.title: 2026 Investment Grade Bond Statistics — InvestmentGrade.com
articles.summary: IG OAS at 77 bps (May 12) — well inside 10-yr avg of ~130 bps. Index yield 5.22%. Q1 2026 issuance $775.2B (largest since Q2 2020, +15.6% YoY). Only 3 US corps rated Aaa (MSFT, JNJ, AAPL). Quality ladder compressed: AAA to BBB yield spread only ~115 bps. BBB tier ~50% of IG market. US sovereign downgraded to Aa1 (May 2025). IG default rate near historic floors. HY projected default rate rising above 4%.

articles.title: CTAs Post Double-Digit Gains in 2026 as Oil Trends Deliver — but Strategies Are Shifting
articles.summary: SG CTA Index +12.2% YTD. SG Trend Index +12.3% YTD. Energy contributed ~1/3 of gains. CTA energy gains since March ~250% the size of losses coming into March. Brent at $93.09 (flat Friday). Gold Aug futures $4,354 (-3.35%). CTAs reducing long energy exposure as price action choppier. Also short across global government bond futures. Risk: broad shift to mean-reversion across many markets.

articles.title: Iran War 2026 — Day 98 Update (GlobalSecurity.org)
articles.summary: First pause in direct IRGC-US kinetic exchanges since May 30-31 escalatory cycle. No new CENTCOM strikes or IRGC launches. Hezbollah rejected US-brokered ceasefire — Israeli strikes killed 4 in Lebanon. Trump: Strait of Hormuz deal possible 'over the next week.' Red line: killing US troops. If talks fail: blockade continues, no bombing. Trump disclosed an abandoned plan to send troops to seize Iran's uranium stockpile.

articles.title: SpaceX Targets Fixed $135 IPO Price at $1.77 Trillion Valuation — CNBC
articles.summary: SpaceX fixed $135/share price. 555.6M shares = $75B fundraise (+$11.2B greenshoe). $1.75T valuation. Nasdaq listing June 12 as SPCX. Musk retains 82%+ voting control. Goldman Sachs lead, 21-bank syndicate. Largest IPO ever (>3x Alibaba). Would rank 7th-largest US company. Anthropic confidentially filed. OpenAI preparing. Cumulative IPO wave could demand >$200B. Governance concern: dual-class structure limits shareholder influence.
